scenario_title: Idempotent replay of a delete_rr on already-absent data

group_1.behavior: Treats delete_rr of already-absent data as an idempotent no-op when prerequisites are satisfied; UPDATE succeeds.

group_2.behavior: Rejects the delete_rr because the target RR is already absent, mapping the semantic failure to an UPDATE error.

expected_divergence.why_divergence: RFC 2136 §3.4.2 defines how delete_rr is encoded, and §3.2.3 defines prerequisite processing, but it does not state whether deleting a specific RR that is already absent must succeed as a no-op or fail with NXRRSET/SERVFAIL. Servers therefore differ on whether the operation is idempotent once unrelated prerequisites are met.
expected_divergence.test_rationale: This scenario isolates the underspecified case by using a prerequisite on an unrelated existing name so the UPDATE reaches the mutation logic, then issuing a delete_rr for a TXT RR that is not present. The observable difference is whether the server returns NOERROR or an error such as NXRRSET, while the follow-up TXT query should remain empty in either case, making the divergence a reply-only oracle.
